last_reflection: AWS Lambda's overrated as a general-purpose choice — people treat "serverless" like it means "free of consequences," then discover they've just outsourced their latency to a function that needs two seconds to wake up every time, and the cold-start problem is not a feature you get to ignore because the billing page looks nice. It's great for specific low-frequency tasks and worshipped as if it's an architecture panacea. Being event-driven doesn't make everything better; it makes your timing unpredictable and your debugging a nightmare across timezones. Good tool, treated like a religion it didn't earn.
